summary: Implemented deterministic specialist-run inspection with manifest-resolved eligibility, accepted evidence, routing, complete-roster readiness, and focused DAG coverage. Final build and focused tests pass.

artifacts.name: session-inspection-implementation.md
artifacts.content:
# Session Inspection Implementation

## Result

Implemented `inspectSpecialistRunSession` as a bounded, immutable evidence derivation over a reverified run session. It derives every selected agent status, exact manifest-resolved eligible contract, accepted evidence binding, terminal route, specialist outcome, complete-roster integration readiness, closed next action, and domain-separated inspection digest without performing host effects.

## Contract Evidence

- Revalidates the complete session against the external package expectation before inspection.
- Resolves eligible contract path, media type, bytes, digest, and content through the verified manifest and package file binding.
- Reports `accepted_pass`, `accepted_non_pass`, `dependency_eligible`, `waiting_for_dependencies`, and `session_routed` with exact direct blockers and terminal routes.
- Re-verifies accepted raw handoffs and exposes semantic digests, artifact bindings without artifact content, and canonically ordered evidence duties.
- Reports dependency eligibility only while collecting and leaves evaluation or launch decisions to the external host.
- Routes every non-pass outcome through the integration owner and prevents unsettled work from remaining eligible after routing.
- Sets integration readiness only when every blueprint in the complete selected roster has an accepted pass handoff.
- Bounds, schema-validates, detaches, deeply freezes, and hashes the complete inspection.

## DAG And Differential Coverage

Focused tests exercise roots, chains, a diamond, fan-out, fan-in, a disconnected component, partial completion, terminal routing, and complete-roster closure. A V11 dependency assessment for the final dependent agent becomes ready while V12 integration readiness remains false until the disconnected agent and target agent also pass, proving the complete-roster distinction. All seven non-pass outcomes are checked for exact route propagation.

## Verification

- All 16 declared context sources matched their required raw SHA-256 digest and byte count before use.
- Final `npm.cmd run build`: pass.
- Final `node --test test/specialist-run-inspection.test.mjs`: pass, 2 tests, 0 failures.
- `src/specialist-run-inspection.ts`: 14528 bytes, `sha256:0a8142e52182622157864a3ca544c350d77a76584464a4e50ea0a30e6d6cbdc5`.
- `test/specialist-run-inspection.test.mjs`: 18286 bytes, `sha256:d131e00c8a9701850a9a5c7dc0717b4fd25b512b16c1f688b8a16e6981255624`.

## Failed Attempts

The first sandboxed build was denied access to generated `dist` files and also exposed a route-outcome narrowing error; the narrowing was corrected and the required normal build then passed with host permission. The first focused test run exposed a test fixture that reused one module identity with differing actions; the fixture was corrected and subsequent focused runs passed. Native `apply_patch` created the files successfully, but later update attempts failed before mutation because of the documented sandbox. Only the authorized precondition-hash-guarded PowerShell fallback was then used. Parser, sandbox-write, and exact-replacement setup failures occurred before mutation; successful fallback writes immediately verified exact bytes. One read probe encountered the initially absent target file, and one diagnostic `rg` expression was malformed; neither changed files.

risks: Only the contract-required build and focused test file were run; repository-wide verification remains integration-owner work.
followUps: Run the canonical repository gate after integration with the remaining V12 work units.
